title: US and Iran Reach Peace Deal to End Conflict
subtitle: The agreement promises to reopen key shipping routes and stabilize global energy markets.

summary.en: The United States and Iran have reached a preliminary agreement to end their recent war. A formal signing ceremony is scheduled for Friday at a resort in Switzerland. The deal allows Iran to resume oil sales if it keeps to strict conditions. Global markets are reacting with optimism as the Strait of Hormuz begins to reopen. Experts suggest this deal could bring significant economic relief to countries in Africa.
summary.zh: 美國與伊朗已達成一項初步協議以結束近期的戰爭。正式簽署儀式預計於週五在瑞士的一處度假勝地舉行。該協議允許伊朗在遵守嚴格條件的前提下恢復石油銷售。隨著荷莫茲海峽開始重新開放，全球市場反應樂觀。專家認為，此協議可能為非洲國家帶來顯著的經濟紓困。

paragraphs.en: The United States and Iran have reached a preliminary agreement to end their recent conflict, marking a major shift in international relations. According to The Guardian World, the two nations are set to sign a formal peace deal this Friday at a mountainside resort in Switzerland. This site was chosen because it is easy to secure and difficult to access. The agreement aims to stop the fighting and address long-standing tensions between the two countries.
paragraphs.zh: 美國與伊朗已達成一項初步協議，旨在結束近期的衝突，這標誌著國際關係的重大轉變。根據《衛報》國際版（The Guardian World）報導，兩國預計於本週五在瑞士的一處山區度假勝地簽署正式和平協議。選擇該地點是因為當地易於維安且難以進入。此協議旨在停止戰鬥，並解決兩國之間長期存在的緊張局勢。

paragraphs.en: As part of the deal, the United States will allow Iran to immediately start selling oil and fuel again. However, The Guardian World reports that Iran must follow specific rules to keep this right. These conditions include ensuring the free flow of ships through the Strait of Hormuz and promising not to build a nuclear weapon. Iranian deputy foreign minister Majid Takht-Ravanchi stated that a two-month naval blockade on Iranian ports has already been lifted in preparation for the formal signing.
paragraphs.zh: 作為協議的一部分，美國將允許伊朗立即恢復石油與燃料的銷售。然而，《衛報》國際版指出，伊朗必須遵守特定規則才能保有此權利。這些條件包括確保船隻能自由通行荷莫茲海峽，並承諾不製造核子武器。伊朗外交部副部長塔赫特-拉凡奇（Majid Takht-Ravanchi）表示，為準備正式簽署，針對伊朗港口長達兩個月的海上封鎖已經解除。

paragraphs.en: President Donald Trump has expressed support for the plan, noting that he expects the second stage of the deal to move forward quickly. He plans to send the full text of the agreement to the US Congress for review. While the deal is moving forward, it remains a topic of debate. Some politicians and lawmakers have expressed concern about providing financial relief to Iran. Additionally, Hezbollah has suggested that Iran will push for Israel to pull its troops from southern Lebanon during the next phase of talks with Washington.
paragraphs.zh: 川普總統對此計畫表示支持，並指出他預期協議的第二階段能迅速推進。他計畫將協議全文送交美國國會審查。儘管協議正在推進，但仍是爭論焦點。部分政治人物與國會議員對向伊朗提供經濟援助表示擔憂。此外，真主黨（Hezbollah）暗示，在與華盛頓的下一階段談判中，伊朗將推動以色列從黎巴嫩南部撤軍。

paragraphs.en: This peace agreement is already having a clear impact on global markets. Deutsche Welle (DW) Top reports that the prospect of a deal is creating optimism across financial and commodity markets worldwide. The Strait of Hormuz is one of the most important shipping routes in the world, serving as a critical path for global oil supplies. When this route is blocked, energy prices typically rise. Now that the route is reopening, experts expect more stability in the global economy.
paragraphs.zh: 這項和平協議已對全球市場產生顯著影響。德國之聲（Deutsche Welle）報導指出，達成協議的前景正為全球金融與商品市場帶來樂觀情緒。荷莫茲海峽是全球最重要的航運路線之一，也是全球石油供應的關鍵路徑。當該路線受阻時，能源價格通常會上漲。隨著路線重新開放，專家預期全球經濟將更趨穩定。

paragraphs.en: For many countries in Africa, the deal is seen as a major relief. Deutsche Welle (DW) Top explains that lower oil prices and smoother trade flows could help economies that are struggling with high costs for imported goods. Many nations in East Africa, North Africa, and the Sahel rely heavily on imports of energy, fertilizers, and food. Because these goods often travel through the Strait of Hormuz, the recent conflict caused significant price increases and supply chain problems.
paragraphs.zh: 對於許多非洲國家而言，這項協議被視為一大解脫。德國之聲解釋，油價下跌與貿易流動順暢，有助於那些正為進口商品高昂成本所苦的經濟體。東非、北非與薩赫勒地區的許多國家高度依賴能源、肥料與糧食進口。由於這些貨物通常經由荷莫茲海峽運輸，近期的衝突導致了嚴重的價格上漲與供應鏈問題。

paragraphs.en: Samuel Nyandemo, an economics professor at the University of Nairobi, described the news as the best update for Africa in a long time. He noted that the disruption of the shipping route forced many businesses to take long, expensive detours. With the route opening again, transport costs are expected to fall, which could help lower inflation. In Kenya, for example, high fuel prices have caused significant political pressure, and lower oil costs could help ease the burden on the public.
paragraphs.zh: 奈洛比大學（University of Nairobi）經濟學教授尼安德莫（Samuel Nyandemo）形容這則新聞是非洲許久以來最好的消息。他指出，航運路線的中斷迫使許多企業必須繞道而行，不僅耗時且成本高昂。隨著路線重新開放，運輸成本預計將下降，這有助於降低通貨膨脹。以肯亞為例，高昂的燃料價格已造成巨大的政治壓力，油價下跌將有助於減輕民眾負擔。

paragraphs.en: While the war is coming to an end, the situation remains complex. Al Jazeera English reports that the conflict has caused significant damage to Iran’s relationship with its neighbors in the Gulf. Both sides are claiming victory, even though the war came at a very high cost. The next steps will involve two months of final negotiations, which are scheduled to begin immediately after the deal is signed on Friday. These talks will focus on the long-term plan for lifting economic sanctions—which are official penalties placed on a country—and deciding the future of Iran’s nuclear program.
paragraphs.zh: 雖然戰爭即將結束，但情勢依然複雜。半島電視台英語頻道（Al Jazeera English）報導，這場衝突已對伊朗與波斯灣鄰國的關係造成重大損害。儘管戰爭代價高昂，雙方仍各自宣稱勝利。接下來的步驟將涉及為期兩個月的最終談判，預計於週五簽署協議後立即展開。這些談判將聚焦於解除經濟制裁（即對一國實施的官方懲罰措施）的長期計畫，並決定伊朗核子計畫的未來。

paragraphs.en: As the world watches the signing ceremony in Switzerland, the focus will shift to how well the agreement is followed. The success of the deal depends on both sides keeping their promises regarding the Strait of Hormuz and the nuclear program. For now, the global community is looking toward a future with more stable energy prices and a potential end to the recent period of uncertainty.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著全球關注在瑞士舉行的簽署儀式，焦點將轉向協議的執行情況。協議的成功取決於雙方是否能信守關於荷莫茲海峽與核子計畫的承諾。目前，國際社會正期待一個能源價格更穩定、且近期不確定時期可能終結的未來。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make a place safe and protected from unauthorized access.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（安全的），這裡作動詞用。文中：This site was chosen because it is easy to secure and difficult to access.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To officially end a rule, restriction, or blockade.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（舉起），這裡指解除封鎖。文中：Iranian deputy foreign minister Majid Takht-Ravanchi stated that a two-month naval blockade on Iranian ports has already been lifted in preparation for the formal signing.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Financial or material assistance given to those in need or experiencing difficulty.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（鬆一口氣），這裡指經濟上的援助。文中：Some politicians and lawmakers have expressed concern about providing financial relief to Iran.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Longer or less direct routes taken to avoid a blocked or difficult path.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（繞道），這裡作名詞用。文中：He noted that the disruption of the shipping route forced many businesses to take long, expensive detours.

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Move Toward Peace with New Preliminary Deal
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States and Iran have reached a preliminary agreement to end their recent conflict. A formal signing ceremony is scheduled for Friday at a remote resort in Switzerland. The deal focuses on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and managing Iran's nuclear program. However, many American lawmakers are expressing concern because they have not yet seen the full details. Further negotiations will take place over the next two months to finalize the terms.

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Reach Historic Peace Agreement to End Conflict
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States and Iran have officially reached a peace agreement to end their three-month conflict. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced the deal, which mandates an immediate stop to military operations on all fronts. President Donald Trump confirmed the agreement and ordered the end of a naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. International leaders from Europe and the Middle East have expressed strong support for the breakthrough. A formal signing ceremony is scheduled to take place in Switzerland on June 19.

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Reach Peace Deal to End Conflict
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States and Iran have officially reached a peace agreement to end their three-month conflict. Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced the deal, which includes a permanent stop to military operations. President Donald Trump confirmed the agreement and ordered an end to the US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. Despite recent tensions caused by Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon, both sides are moving toward a formal signing ceremony. The agreement is expected to restore global shipping routes and address concerns regarding nuclear materials.

quiz.question: What is one specific condition Iran must meet to maintain its right to sell oil and fuel?

options.A: It must immediately withdraw all its troops from southern Lebanon.
options.B: It must ensure that ships can travel through the Strait of Hormuz without interference.
options.C: It must sign a separate treaty with its neighbors in the Gulf.
options.D: It must agree to pay for the damages caused by the two-month naval blockade.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，美國同意伊朗恢復石油銷售的條件之一，就是伊朗必須確保船隻能自由通行荷姆茲海峽（Strait of Hormuz）。選項 A 是 Hezbollah 的提議，而非伊朗必須遵守的條件；選項 C 文中未提及；選項 D 雖然提到封鎖已解除，但並未要求伊朗支付賠償。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why is the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz particularly beneficial for African nations?

options.A: Because these nations are directly involved in the negotiations between the US and Iran.
options.B: Because they are now able to sell their own oil reserves to the Middle East more easily.
options.C: Because their economies rely on imports that are transported through this specific shipping route.
options.D: Because they have been providing military support to Iran during the conflict.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段與第五段，荷姆茲海峽是全球關鍵的石油運輸路徑，而第五段指出東非與北非國家高度依賴能源、肥料與食物的進口，且這些貨物通常經由該海峽運輸。因此，海峽重啟能降低運輸成本與通膨壓力。選項 A、B、D 皆無事實根據，且與文中提到的非洲經濟困境無關。

quiz.question: In the seventh paragraph, what does the word 'penalties' refer to in the context of economic sanctions?

options.A: Financial rewards given to countries for good behavior.
options.B: Punishments imposed on a nation to influence its political or economic actions.
options.C: Fees that must be paid to international courts during a trial.
options.D: The cost of repairing damage caused by military conflict.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中提到「economic sanctions—which are official penalties placed on a country」，這裡的 penalties 指的是為了迫使對方改變行為而施加的懲罰性限制。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 C 與 D 雖然與錢有關，但並非「制裁（sanctions）」在國際政治中的定義。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main focus of the article?

options.A: A detailed history of the long-standing tensions between the US and Iran.
options.B: The political challenges President Trump faces regarding his foreign policy.
options.C: The global economic and geopolitical implications of the US-Iran peace agreement.
options.D: The specific logistical details of the signing ceremony in Switzerland.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文涵蓋了協議的內容、對全球能源市場的影響、對非洲經濟的幫助，以及未來談判的複雜性，旨在全面探討此和平協議帶來的廣泛影響。選項 A 僅是背景；選項 B 僅是文中提到的一小部分；選項 D 僅是文章開頭的細節，無法涵蓋全文主旨。
